markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose UserManual from editor, Loom, retrieval debug, diagnostics, and command palette. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualInAppAccess leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualInAppAccess is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: UserManual backend/runtime registration and doc-vs-runtime consistency base for MT-193..208
UserManual UI access wiring for editor command palette, diagnostics tab, and panel access surfaces

evidence.summary: MT-200/205 UserManual access is wired through real UI/API surfaces; duplicate selector hardening keeps canonical UserManual diagnostics IDs unique while preserving backend stable IDs for access rows.

commands.result: PASS; 32/32 tests passed for UserManual UI/API access

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-205 selector hardening: canonical hs-usermanual-diagnostics-tab only on pane-A UserManual tab; noncanonical panes use pane-specific IDs; access rows expose stable backend IDs separately.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-200 requires UserManual to be reachable in-app from the five named host surfaces (editor, Notes/Loom, retrieval debug, diagnostics, command palette). The code delivers a typed backend access-point registry covering all five surfaces, a live /usermanual/access-points API that resolves every target page against the real PostgreSQL store, and real frontend wiring (UserManualPanel tab, diagnostics manual tab, command-palette open/search commands, deep links), with real-PG and Vitest proof. It aligns with current Master Spec 10.15.8.
findings: registry.rs:689-762 defines ACCESS_POINTS with 8 entries spanning all 5 MT-named host surfaces (editor, notes_loom, retrieval_debug, diagnostics, command_palette), each with stable_element_id (hs-usermanual-*), target_page_slug, and ui_wiring_route. registry.rs:815-840 test asserts all 5 surfaces present and slug/route invariants.

api/user_manual.rs:466-489 access_points handler resolves each access point's target_page_slug against the LIVE UserManualStore (get_page_by_slug) and returns target_resolves, so a dangling access point is a runtime-detected defect, not a static claim. Route mounted at /usermanual/access-points (line 63).

tests/user_manual_api_tests.rs:424-461 mt200_access_points_resolve hits the real HTTP route, asserts target_resolves==true for every row and that all 5 host surfaces are covered. tests/user_manual_support.rs:96-118 builds AppState on a real PostgresDatabase::connect to an isolated schema; skip_if_no_pg! (support.rs:133-142) PANICS when PG is absent rather than silently skipping, so the proof genuinely exercises managed PostgreSQL (sub-rule 2 satisfied). Noop recorders are only for flight-recorder/diagnostics/LLM, not the manual store.

Frontend wiring is real end-to-end: App.tsx:45,1235-1249,1475-1481 mount UserManualPanel as a workspace tab, openUserManualPane callback, and palette handlers for usermanual.open/usermanual.search; lib/app_command_registry.ts:73-94 registers the two palette commands; App.tsx:323 + 1849 wire the canonical diagnostics manual tab stable id hs-usermanual-diagnostics-tab on pane-A. Covered by App.test.tsx, UserManualPanel.test.tsx, api.test.ts.

No deferred-live escapes or code placeholders in the MT-200 surfaces; the only 'placeholder' hit (UserManualPanel.tsx:226) is an HTML input placeholder attribute. No forbidden tech: durable state is PostgreSQL only, no SQLite, no external search engine.

Aside (non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 anchors; spec 10.15.8 exists in current v02.194 and the implementation matches it (in-app manual view + diagnostics manual tab + command surfaces resolving against UserManual authority).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
